variant: compare 2 acne treatment options — include timeframes like 'week 4 — visible change' in the details

request.message: compare 2 acne treatment options — include timeframes like 'week 4 — visible change' in the details

response_body.response:
**best move for you:** stack them. azelaic AM, tretinoin PM.

azelaic handles bacteria + redness while tretinoin remodels skin at night. week 4 you'll see the combo working; week 6–8 is where acne noticeably clears.

start tretinoin low (0.05%, pea-sized on dry skin) and ramp slowly to avoid purge. never apply both the same night.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: Differin Adapalene Gel 0.1%
products.description: OTC retinoid (adapalene 0.1%) — first-line for comedonal acne.

visual_blocks.title: acne treatment: azelaic acid vs tretinoin

options.name: azelaic acid (AM)
pros: week 3–4, visible acne improvement, less irritation
cons: slower than tretinoin for severe acne
requires consistent use for full effect
may need 6–8 weeks for max results

options.name: tretinoin 0.05% (PM)
pros: week 4–6, visible cell turnover, acne clearing
strongest retinoid, real results on texture
drives pigmented acne scars to shed
cons: requires dry skin application, pea-sized only
cannot use same night as other strong actives
steeper learning curve, needs barrier support
